title: The Little Engine That Could
content:
A little train full of toys was going over a mountain to bring presents to the children on the other side.

But oh no! The little train broke down.

"Please help me," said the little train to a shiny new engine. "I must get these toys to the children."

"I cannot pull so many toys," said the shiny engine. "I am too important." And he went away.

A big strong engine came by. "Can you help me?" asked the little train.

"I don't pull toys," said the big engine. "I pull passenger cars." And he puffed away.

Then a small blue engine came along.

"Will you help me?" asked the little train.

The small blue engine was not very big. She had never been over the mountain before.

But she said, "I think I can. I think I can."

She was hooked to the little train. She started up the mountain.

"I think I can. I think I can. I think I can," she puffed.

The mountain was steep. The toys were heavy.

"I think I can. I think I can. I think I can."

Finally, she reached the top!

"I thought I could! I thought I could!" she said happily.

Down the other side she went, all the way to the children.

The children cheered! They got their toys because one little engine believed she could.

The End.
